More Notes: The study analyzes the average employment impact across industries after hurricanes; 2007 is the midpoint of the 1995-2019 study period and can serve as a representative year.
Average decline in overall employment after six months
Average weekly wages increase after six months, but the increase is not statistically significant.
The average hurricane in the dataset causes a 0.5% decline in overall employment. Average of the period 1995-2019 is taken as 2007
Construction is among the industries classified as 'strengthened' in response to hurricanes. 1 = Strengthened
Furniture is among the industries classified as 'strengthened' in response to hurricanes. 1 = Strengthened
Crop production is among the industries classified as 'weakened' in response to hurricanes. -1 = Weakened
Retail is among the industries classified as 'weakened' in response to hurricanes. -1 = Weakened
Average decline in overall employment after hurricane (short-run); 2007 is the midpoint of 1995-2019
Refers to U.S. county employment increase, sourced from Strobl and Walsh(2009)
Refers to increase in construction employment one year after all kinds of disasters, sourced from Roth Tran and Wilson(2021)
